issues.claim_in_outline: Section I: 60 MCQ / 90 min / 50%; Section II: 6 FRQ / 90 min / 50% — 2 long FRQs 8–10 pts each (interpret an experiment) + 4 short FRQs 4 pts each (analyze data / build-use model / predict).
issues.description: Every exam-format number matches the reference exactly: 60 MCQ, 90 min, 50%; 6 FRQ, 90 min, 50%; 2 long FRQs at 8–10 pts each; 4 short FRQs at 4 pts each.

issues.claim_in_outline: Scored 1–5; a 3 or higher is a qualifying score that earns college credit at many institutions.
issues.description: Matches reference: '1-5 scale; 3+ qualifies for college credit at many institutions.' The added caveat that each college sets its own policy is not contradicted by the reference.

issues.claim_in_outline: Six science practices: 1 concept explanation, 2 visual representation, 3 question/method, 4 data representation, 5 statistical tests, 6 argument from evidence.
issues.description: All six named science practices match the reference's list exactly and in the same order. Parenthetical examples (e.g., chi-square, error bars, IV/DV/control) are illustrative and consistent with the named practices.

issues.claim_in_outline: 10% rule (~10% energy up, ~90% lost as heat; energy flows, matter cycles); Hardy-Weinberg null model with 5 assumptions; correlation vs causation; exponential vs logistic.
issues.description: Cross-unit trap recaps. None state a number or definition that contradicts the reference's listed common pitfalls (correlation/causation, 10% rule, exponential vs logistic, H-W assumptions). Treated as in-scope course material.

issues.claim_in_outline: Recap repeats all exam-format numbers and the six practices.
issues.description: Recap restates 60/90/50, 6 FRQ/90/50, 2 long 8–10 pts, 4 short 4 pts, 1–5, 3+, and the six practices — all consistent with the reference and with earlier sections.

overall_assessment: All exam-format claims (60 MCQ/90 min/50%; 6 FRQ/90 min/50%; 2 long FRQs at 8–10 pts each; 4 short FRQs at 4 pts each; 1–5 scale; 3+ qualifying) match the reference exactly. The six science practices match the reference's named list exactly and in order. Cross-unit trap recaps are in-scope and do not contradict the reference's listed pitfalls. No unsupported numbers, timings, weightings, or science-practice names found. Verdict: pass.
